title: Java Methods
content:
### Methods in Java

A method is a block of code that runs when called. It can accept parameters and return values.

---

### Method Declaration

```java
accessModifier returnType methodName(parameterList) {
    // method body
    return value;
}
```

---

### Method Components

| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| Access Modifier | `public`, `private`, `protected`, default |
| Return Type | Data type of return value (or `void`) |
| Method Name | camelCase naming |
| Parameters | Input values (optional) |
| Body | Code to execute |

---

### Types of Methods

| Type | Description |
|------|-------------|
| Instance method | Belongs to an object |
| Static method | Belongs to the class |
| Getter/Setter | Access private fields |
| Abstract method | No body, must be overridden |
| Final method | Cannot be overridden |

---

### Parameter Passing

Java passes primitives by value and object references by value:

```java
public void modifyValue(int x) {
    x = 10;  // original variable unchanged
}

public void modifyArray(int[] arr) {
    arr[0] = 99;  // original array modified
}
```

---

### Varargs (Variable Arguments)

```java
public static void printNumbers(int... numbers) {
    for (int n : numbers) {
        System.out.println(n);
    }
}
```

> **Key Point:** Methods must be declared inside a class. Return type is mandatory (use `void` if nothing returned). Java always passes arguments by value.

quiz.question: What keyword is used when a method returns nothing?
Does Java pass objects by value or reference?
